info.name: Time
info.comment: Simplifies the management of date/time information.

description.p: The Time class is available for programs that require time and date management in a multi-platform manner.
To get the current system time, use the Query action.

action.name: Query
action.comment: Updates the values in a time object with the current system date and time.

action.name: Refresh
action.comment: Refreshes the state of an object.

method.name: GetTimeZoneInfo
method.comment: Returns time-zone metadata and transition records.
method.prototype: ERR pt::GetTimeZoneInfo(OBJECTPTR Object, CSTRING ZoneID, INT StartYear, INT EndYear, struct TimeZoneInfo ** Info)

input.param: Empty or NULL requests the local system zone.
Inclusive first year. Must be in the supported 1601-9999 range.
Inclusive final year. Must be >= StartYear, no later than 9999 and within 400 years of StartYear.
Receives the allocated metadata and transition resource. Release with FreeResource when no longer required.

description.p: Returns normalised time-zone information for a named zone or for the host system's local zone. The returned TimeZoneInfo resource includes the preferred public zone identifier, the host-native identifier, the data source, standard UTC offset and any transition records found within the requested inclusive year range.

The ZoneID value may be NULL or empty to request the local system zone. Explicit zone IDs support UTC aliases on all platforms. Linux builds also accept IANA zone names that resolve under /usr/share/zoneinfo. Windows builds accept Windows native time-zone IDs and the IANA IDs that are mapped by the Core Windows time-zone wrapper.

For explicit zone requests, an unknown or unsupported ID returns ERR::Search. For local-zone requests, host lookup failure is not fatal; the method returns a UTC result with Source set to utc and IsFallback set to 1.

Transition Instant values are UTC Unix epoch timestamps in microseconds. OffsetBefore, OffsetAfter and BaseOffset are expressed in seconds east of UTC. DaylightSaving is 1 when the transition enters a daylight-saving period.

result.error: Time-zone information was returned. Local-zone failures may return a UTC fallback result.
An explicit zone ID could not be resolved.
A value is outside of the valid range
AllocMemory() failed to create a new memory block
A required host time-zone API call failed.

method.name: SetTime
method.comment: Apply the time to the system clock.

description.p: This method will apply the time object's values to the BIOS. Depending on the host platform, this method may only work if the user is logged in as the administrator.

field.name: DayOfWeek
field.comment: Day of week (0 - 6) starting from Sunday.

field.name: MicroSecond
field.comment: A microsecond is one millionth of a second (0 - 999999)

field.name: MilliSecond
field.comment: A millisecond is one thousandth of a second (0 - 999)

field.name: SystemTime
field.comment: Represents the system time when the time object was last queried.

description.p: The SystemTime field returns the system time if the Time object has been queried. The time is represented in microseconds. This field serves no purpose beyond its initial query value.

field.name: Timestamp
field.comment: Read this field to get representation of the time as a single integer.

description.p: The Timestamp field is a 64-bit integer that represents the time object as an approximation of the number of milliseconds represented in the time object (approximately the total amount of time passed since Zero-AD). This is convenient for summarising a time value for comparison with other time stamps, or for storing time in a 64-bit space.
The Timestamp value is dynamically calculated when reading this field.

struct.name: TimeZoneInfo
struct.comment: Time zone information returned from GetTimeZoneInfo()
struct.field: Preferred public ID, e.g. Europe/London or UTC.
Host-native ID, e.g. GMT Standard Time on Windows.
TZif path on Linux, otherwise empty.
TZDB/source version if available, otherwise empty.
Transitions available for the requested range.
1 if ZoneID requested the local system zone.
